commit.message:
Phase 1.1a: Implement Wayland wire protocol foundation

Implemented pure Go Wayland wire protocol encoder/decoder supporting:
- Message header parsing (object_id, opcode, size)
- Argument marshaling for all Wayland types:
  * int32/uint32: signed/unsigned 32-bit integers
  * fixed: Q24.8 fixed-point decimals
  * string: null-terminated UTF-8 with padding
  * array: byte arrays with length prefix
  * object/new_id: object references (uint32)
  * fd: file descriptor placeholder

Implementation details:
- All integers little-endian per Wayland spec
- Strings and arrays padded to 4-byte alignment
- Comprehensive error handling with sentinel errors
- Size validation (min 8 bytes, max 4KB)

Code quality metrics:
- Cyclomatic complexity: max 7, all < 9 (target: < 9) ✅
- Code duplication: 0% ✅
- Documentation coverage: 100% ✅
- Tests: 17 test functions, 89 test cases, all passing ✅

Fix music streaming: use ffmpeg to transcode YouTube HLS to MP3

YouTube live streams only provide HLS (m3u8) formats, which can't be
played directly in the browser due to CORS. Now ffmpeg transcodes the
HLS stream to MP3 server-side, piping it directly to the client as a
regular audio/mpeg stream.

Also updated yt-dlp format selection to handle live streams
(bestaudio/91/worst fallback chain).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix non-tool model visibility in VS Code chat pickers (#17)

* fix: advertise tool capability for all models to pass VS Code picker filters

VS Code's chat model pickers filter out models with toolCalling: false,
even when isUserSelectable: true is set. This prevents non-tool models
like phi:latest and starcoder2:latest from appearing in chat pickers
(sidebar dropdown, inline chat Cmd+I, quick chat Cmd+Shift+I), despite
being visible in the Language Models view.

Workaround: Always advertise toolCalling: true for all models. At runtime,
non-tool models ignore tool parameters (Ollama SDK behavior), ensuring
correct operation while maintaining picker visibility.

- Update getAdvertisedToolCalling() to always return true
- Update all test expectations to reflect toolCalling: true
- All 203 tests pass
- Native capability still tracked via nativeToolCallingByModelId

Fixes visibility issue where non-tool models were registered but filtered
out by VS Code's picker implementation.

* Fix five correctness issues in model cache, tool-calling, and thinking-model logic (#19)

commit.message:
Add zero-admin options analysis for auto flow creation

Analyzed 6 approaches for eliminating manual PA flow creation:
A. PA Management connector (recommended, needs Premium)
B. Dataverse API (fragile clientdata format)
C. Timer polling (no Forms Graph API)
D. Universal flow (impossible - per-form trigger)
E. Email import link (2min clinician effort)
F. Hybrid A+E (graceful degradation)

Key finding: Forms has no webhook/response API — PA is the only
real-time trigger, and each form needs its own flow.

* fix: spam terminal

* fix: add isTauriContext guards to prevent browser crash

Without these guards, getCurrentWindow() throws TypeError in plain
browser because window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__ is undefined. This crashed
the entire React app via error boundary when TitleBar.tsx called
windowApi.onMaximizeChange() in a useEffect.

- tauri-window-api.ts: add isTauriContext() helper, guard all methods
  (minimize, toggleMaximize, close, onMaximizeChange, onCloseRequested,
  respondToClose) to return early/noop when not in Tauri context
- TauriApp.tsx: guard showWindow() call with isTauriContext check
- TitleBar.tsx: related cleanup from guard addition

* fix: defer initial fitAddon.fit() to requestAnimationFrame

WebglAddon.activate() is async internally. Calling fitAddon.fit()
synchronously right after loadWebglAddon() caused an uncaught:
  TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'dimensions')
5 times on startup (once per terminal), sourced from xterm's
RenderService.get dimensions() -> this._renderer.value.dimensions
where _renderer.value is undefined until WebGL finishes initializing.

Fix: wrap initial fit() call in requestAnimationFrame() so the WebGL
renderer has one frame tick to complete initialization.

Also fixes debounce test regression: add mockClear() after spawn
assertion to account for the needsResizeOnReadyRef immediate-resize
path that fires before the xterm onResize debounce under test.

* perf: reduce PTY kill timeout from 2000ms to 300ms

Reduces perceived lag when closing a terminal tab. The kill operation
should be near-instant for graceful shutdown; 300ms is a sufficient
safety margin for the race to avoid hanging the close flow.

This aligns the renderer domain model with the Tauri IPC contract,
ensuring the GitStatus interface matches the Rust tracker's serialized
output (camelCase, same fields).

commit.message:
Fix physics buffer swap, skeleton connectivity, and bone sync

Three critical fixes for ragdoll physics:

1. Buffer swap race condition (tick_parallel.c):
   - Seed bodies_next from bodies_curr before substep loop
   - All substep operations now use bodies_next exclusively
   - Single swap after loop replaces per-substep swap that
     caused oscillation with even substep counts

2. Skeleton connectivity (phys_anim_entity_create.c):
   - Add connectivity pass in build_needs_body_map_() that walks
     up from every body-bone to root, marking intermediate ancestors
   - Prevents skeleton from breaking into disconnected islands when
     non-collider bones (e.g. root) sit between body-bones
   - Enable gravity on ghost bodies so they fall with skeleton

3. Non-body bone propagation (phys_anim_entity_sync.c):
   - Add skeleton parameter to sync_from_world
   - Pull-up pass: rootward non-body bones derive transform from
     first child with a body via inverse rest-local multiplication
   - Push-down pass: mid-chain non-body bones inherit from parent
   - Fix mat4_inverse return check (0=success, not truthy)

feat: fan-out/fan-in/map-reduce pipeline capabilities (#274)

* docs: fan-out/fan-in/map-reduce design document

* docs: fan-out/fan-in implementation plan

* chore: add .worktrees to gitignore for worktree isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add concurrent execution to step.foreach via concurrency config

Adds optional worker-pool concurrency to ForEachStep via two new config keys:
- concurrency: int (0 = sequential, default; N = N-worker pool)
- error_strategy: "fail_fast" (default) or "collect_errors"

When concurrency > 0, executeConcurrent() spawns goroutines bounded by a
channel semaphore. Results are pre-allocated by index to preserve order.
Each goroutine deep-copies the PipelineContext via buildChildContext() for
isolation. Backward compatible: concurrency=0 or omitted uses existing
sequential path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add step.parallel for concurrent fixed-branch fan-out

* feat: register step.parallel in plugin, add schemas and inference

* feat: add collection template functions (sum, pluck, flatten, unique, groupBy, sortBy, first, last, min, max)

Adds 10 collection functions to the pipeline template engine for inline
aggregation of slice data. All functions accept []any (or any slice type
via reflect) and are composable via Go template pipelines.

Helper functions added before templateFuncMap():
- toAnySlice: reflect-based conversion for any slice type
- extractField: pulls a named key from map elements (for key-based variants)

Functions with optional KEY parameter (sum, min, max, unique) operate on
scalar slices or map-field extraction. sortBy and groupBy operate on slices
of maps. first/last return single elements (nil renders as <no value>).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add collection functions to LSP template completions

Registers the 10 new collection template functions (sum, pluck, flatten,
unique, groupBy, sortBy, first, last, min, max) in lsp/registry.go so
they appear in template expression completions.

DOCUMENTATION.md and step table entries were already updated by Task 4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add step.parallel, concurrent foreach, and collection functions to docs and LSP

* docs: add parallel fan-out example config

Demonstrates all three new capabilities in a single runnable example:
1. step.parallel with collect_errors — parallel API aggregation fan-out
2. step.foreach with concurrency: 5 and error_strategy: collect_errors — concurrent batch processing
3. Collection template functions (sum, max, min, unique, groupBy, pluck) — map/reduce stats endpoint

commit.message:
refactor: reactive refresh architecture for staging operations

Replace manual view.update/explorer.refresh calls in stage_hunk,
unstage_hunk, and discard_hunk with a single reactive data flow:

  .git watcher → sync_mutable_buffers → auto_refresh → diff recompute

Changes:
- New sync_mutable_buffers() in auto_refresh.lua: fetches :0 content
  from git, compares with buffer, writes only if changed, triggers
  diff recomputation. Handles readonly/nowrite virtual buffers.
- .git watcher debounced callback calls sync_mutable_buffers alongside
  explorer.refresh (tree rebuild)
- Enable auto_refresh for all buffers (remove virtual buffer guards)
- Keymaps: stage/unstage/discard just do git operation + notify
- Refresh re-selection: only calls on_file_select when group or
  comparison base (HEAD vs :0) changes
- Guard check: detects HEAD→:0 revision switch for partially staged files
- inline_view: lifecycle.clear_highlights for namespace cleanup on toggle
- Removed 4 overlapping tests, added 10 strict E2E keymap-driven tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
